title: Fixture: Bronze-tier high-criticality UC for tests
description: Detects synthetic hosts reporting elevated CPU using intentionally sloppy SPL for anti-pattern regression tests.
value: Bronze fixtures anchor negative tests: auditors must still reject one-liner pipelines, join truncation, and random() demo values even when metadata looks complete.
app: Fixture Lab Generator (no Splunkbase app — tests only).
spl: | makeresults count=5 | eval host="fixture-"+tostring(mvrange(0,5)), cpu_pct=round(50+random()%50,1) | join host [search index=fixture_inventory | stats latest(site) AS site BY host] | stats avg(cpu_pct) AS avg_cpu BY host | search avg_cpu > 80
implementation: Enable fixture event generator, paste SPL into ad-hoc search, expect anti-pattern linter to flag join/random/makeresults.
grandmaExplanation: This is a fake example we use in automated tests to make sure bad search patterns get caught before they reach real dashboards.
